text: Large language models (LLMs) are advanced AI systems that learn from huge amounts of written text. They work by predicting the next word in a sentence, based on patterns they find in their training data—everything from books and websites to articles. To use them effectively, people often practise prompt engineering: giving clear, specific instructions or questions to get a useful answer. LLMs can help with writing, answering questions, and even creative tasks. However, they have limits. They might create false information, struggle with very recent events, and lack real understanding or common sense. So while they are powerful tools, they are not perfect and need careful use.
